title: ischaemum aristatum subvar. rugosum
intro: Ischaemum aristatum subvar. rugosum, also known as the Rough Ischaemum or Coarse Wild Rye Grass, is a type of grass native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing cholesterol levels, and aiding in digestion, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
